threads: setting up Terraform IaC for multi-region agent deployment
configuring VPC peering between us-east-1 and us-west-2
writing Terraform modules for ECS agent clusters
cost optimization for idle agent containers

messages.content: We need Terraform IaC for multi-region agent deployment. Regions: us-east-1 and us-west-2
Setting up Terraform IaC for multi-region ECS agent deployment. Module path: /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/terraform/modules/agent-cluster/main.tf
Use terraform-v1.8.3 and workspace prod-us-east-1 for the primary region
Using Terraform v1.8.3. Active workspace: prod-us-east-1. Module: /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/terraform/modules/agent-cluster/main.tf
VPC CIDR for us-east-1 is 10.0.0.0/16 and us-west-2 is 10.1.0.0/16
VPC CIDRs: us-east-1 = 10.0.0.0/16, us-west-2 = 10.1.0.0/16. No overlap — VPC peering will work between these.
First thread: write the agent-cluster Terraform module
Writing /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/terraform/modules/agent-cluster/main.tf. Module inputs: region, vpc_cidr, cluster_name, instance_count, environment.
Add ECS Fargate capacity with auto-scaling
Adding ECS Fargate capacity provider to the agent-cluster module. Auto-scaling: min 1, max 20 tasks. Scale up at 70% CPU, scale down at 30%.
Second thread: VPC peering between us-east-1 and us-west-2
Setting up VPC peering resource in Terraform. Peering requester: 10.0.0.0/16 (us-east-1), accepter: 10.1.0.0/16 (us-west-2). Route tables updated on both sides.
How do we handle DNS across VPCs?
Enabling DNS resolution and DNS hostnames on both VPCs, then creating Route 53 private hosted zones with association to both VPCs. Agents in 10.1.0.0/16 can resolve agents.us-east-1.moltaicorp.com.
The health endpoint in us-east-1 is https://agents.us-east-1.moltaicorp.com/health
Health endpoint https://agents.us-east-1.moltaicorp.com/health registered in Route 53 private zone associated with both 10.0.0.0/16 and 10.1.0.0/16 VPCs.
Third thread: ECS module for the agent clusters
The ECS cluster module in /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/terraform/modules/agent-cluster/main.tf creates: ECS cluster, task definition, service, security groups, IAM roles.
Add the task definition for the agent container
Adding ECS task definition to the module. Container: 1 vCPU, 2GB RAM by default, configurable via module variables. Environment pulled from SSM Parameter Store.
Fourth thread: cost optimization. What are the idle containers costing us?
With ECS Fargate, idle containers (0.25 vCPU, 0.5GB) cost ~$11/month each. If we have 10 agent slots, that's $110/month just for idle capacity.
Add scale-to-zero for agent slots idle >15 minutes
Adding scale-to-zero to the Terraform module: EventBridge rule triggers Lambda to check for idle tasks (0 requests in 15 min). Lambda sets ECS service desired_count=0.
How does the first request wake up a scaled-to-zero agent?
Cold start: ALB gets first request, forwards to empty target group. Lambda (triggered by ALB 502) sets desired_count=1, waits for task to register. ~30 second cold start for Fargate.
That's too slow. What about warm standbys?
Alternative: keep 1 warm standby per region (1 task in 10.0.0.0/16 us-east-1, 1 in 10.1.0.0/16 us-west-2). Cold start is eliminated, cost is only $22/month for the two warmstandbys.
Let's do warm standby. Update the Terraform module
Updating /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/terraform/modules/agent-cluster/main.tf: min_capacity=1 (warm standby), scale_up threshold at 70% CPU, scale_down to 1 (not 0).
What's the Terraform workspace for prod?
Prod workspace is prod-us-east-1 using terraform-v1.8.3. Secondary region (us-west-2) will use workspace prod-us-west-2.
Plan the Terraform changes
Running terraform plan in workspace prod-us-east-1. Plan shows: 14 resources to add, 0 to change, 0 to destroy. Includes VPC 10.0.0.0/16, peering to 10.1.0.0/16, ECS cluster, and warm standby.
Apply it
Running terraform apply in workspace prod-us-east-1. All 14 resources applied. https://agents.us-east-1.moltaicorp.com/health returning 200.
Great. Apply us-west-2 next
Switching to workspace prod-us-west-2, applying the agent-cluster module with cidr=10.1.0.0/16. VPC peering accepted from us-east-1.
How much did we save vs the original plan?

Cost comparison: scale-to-zero would have saved most idle cost but 30s cold starts. Warm standby: $22/month for 2 tasks, eliminates cold starts. vs. always-on: $110/month for 10 idle tasks. Saving $88/month.
Document the cost model

Documenting cost model in Terraform module README: warm standby ($22/mo) vs. always-on ($110/mo) vs. scale-to-zero ($0 idle, 30s cold). Using warm standby as default in /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/terraform/modules/agent-cluster/main.tf.
What were the four threads today?

Four threads: 1) Terraform module for agent clusters at /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/repos/molt-infra/terraform/modules/agent-cluster/main.tf, 2) VPC peering (10.0.0.0/16 ↔ 10.1.0.0/16), 3) ECS Fargate with task definitions, 4) Cost optimization (warm standby pattern). All applied via prod-us-east-1 workspace.
Commit and push
Committed and pushed terraform-v1.8.3 compatible IaC. Both regions deployed. https://agents.us-east-1.moltaicorp.com/health is live.
